McBride PLC
27 May 2026

McBride plc
Transaction in Own Shares
McBride plc (the "
Company
") announces today it has purchased for cancellation the following number of its ordinary shares of 10 pence each ("
Ordinary Shares

Broker

The Company intends to cancel the purchased Ordinary Shares.
Such share purchases form part of the Company's existing buyback programme and were effected pursuant to the non-discretionary agreement entered into with the Broker, as announced on 1 May 2026.
Following the cancellation of the repurchased Ordinary Shares, the Company will have 174,670,078 Ordinary Shares in issue (excluding treasury shares), hold 42,041 Ordinary Shares in treasury, and have 594,610,458
non-cumulative redeemable preference shares of 0.1 pence each in the Company ("
B Shares
") in issue. B Shares carry no rights to attend, speak or vote at Company meetings, except on a resolution relating to the winding up of the Company.
Once the Ordinary Shares are cancelled, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 174,670,078. This figure may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest, or a change to their interest, in the Company pursuant to the Disclosure and Transparency Rules (the "DTRs").
The Company confirms that, in accordance with DTR 5.5.1R, following the cancellation of the repurchased Ordinary Shares the percentage of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares held in treasury will be 0.02 per cent.
Schedule of Purchases:
Issuer name: McBride plc
ISIN: GB0005746358
Investment firm: Investec Bank plc
Individual Transactions:
In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (as it forms part of the law of the United Kingdom by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 as that Act is amended from time to time), a full breakdown of the individual trades made by the Broker on behalf of the Company as part of the buyback programme is set out in the Schedule to this announcement available through the link below:
http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/7495F_1-2026-5-26.pdf

latest.title: Second tranche of share buyback programme
latest.content:
1 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6884C
McBride PLC
01 May 2026
McBride plc
Second tranche of share buyback programme
McBride plc (the "
Company
"), the leading European manufacturer and supplier of private label and contract manufactured products for the domestic household and professional cleaning/hygiene markets, today announces the commencement of the second tranche of its share buyback programme to purchase ordinary shares of 10 pence each in the Company ("
Ordinary Shares
"), as launched on 1 December 2025 (the "
Programme
").
Purchases under the first tranche of the Programme commenced on 1 December 2025 and continued until 30 April 2026. Under the first tranche, between 1 December 2025 and 30 April 2026, the Company repurchased 3,236,565 Ordinary Shares for an aggregate consideration of approximately £4.5 million.
The Company announces that the second tranche of the Programme of up to £15.5 million (being the balance of the £20 million maximum amount of the Programme less the aggregate consideration paid under the first tranche of the Programme) will commence today (the "
Second Tranche
"). The Second Tranche is structured in two phases. In respect of the first phase, the Company has entered into a non-discretionary agreement with Investec Bank plc ("
Investec
") to act as riskless principal in relation to the purchase of Ordinary Shares, and the subsequent sale of such Ordinary Shares to the Company. The second phase will be undertaken by Peel Hunt LLP ("
Peel Hunt
", together with Investec, the "
Brokers
"). The Company has entered into a non-discretionary agreement with Peel Hunt to act as riskless principal in relation to the purchase of Ordinary Shares, and the subsequent sale of such Ordinary Shares to the Company.
Purchases of Ordinary Shares under the Second Tranche are expected to commence on 1 May 2026 and to be completed by no later than 30 September 2026. The Company intends to announce any purchases under the Programme on a weekly basis from the commencement of the Second Tranche of the Programme.
The Brokers will each make trading decisions in relation to the Programme independently of the Company. Any purchase of Ordinary Shares by the Brokers contemplated by this announcement will be carried out on the London Stock Exchange. Any purchases of Ordinary Shares by the Company under the Programme will be carried out on the London Stock Exchange.
The purpose of the Programme is to reduce the share capital of the Company by returning surplus capital to shareholders. Ordinary Shares purchased under the Second Tranche will be cancelled. These arrangements are in accordance with the Company's general authority to repurchase up to 17,401,528 Ordinary Shares granted at its 2025 Annual General Meeting. The Second Tranche will also be conducted within certain pre-set parameters, in accordance with Chapter 9 of the Listing Rules and within the parameters prescribed by the Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014/EU and the Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 (both as incorporated into UK domestic law by the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018).
From time to time, the Board will assess the progress of this Programme in light of the financial situation of the Company and its capital allocation needs.
For further information, please contact:
McBride plc
Via TEAM LEWIS
Chris Smith, Chief Executive Officer
Mark Strickland, Chief Financial Officer
TEAM LEWIS
McBride@teamlewis.com
Hannah Scott

company_30s.what_they_do: McBride plc is a UK‑based manufacturer of cleaning and hygiene products.  It supplies a mix of branded and private‑label solutions to supermarkets, retailers and the commercial sector.  Products range from household detergents to industrial disinfectants and solid‑format cleaning tools.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The cleaning‑and‑hygiene market is a staple of everyday life and a core part of the retail supply chain.  McBride’s long‑term contracts with major supermarkets give it a stable revenue base, while its private‑label portfolio offers margin upside.  In an economy where hygiene remains a priority, the company sits in a resilient niche with room for scale.
company_30s.current_state: McBride is in a period of active capital management and portfolio expansion.  It has launched a second tranche of a share‑buyback programme, announced the purchase of Eurotab Group to add solid‑format cleaning solutions, and recently added a new director.  These moves signal a push to strengthen cash flow and broaden product offerings.  The share price sits near the bottom of its 52‑week range, suggesting a possible undervaluation relative to its historical valuation multiples.

rns_narrative: The company is returning cash to shareholders through a staged buyback, signalling confidence in its cash‑flow outlook.
The acquisition of Eurotab Group is aimed at diversifying the product mix and capturing a new segment of the cleaning market.
A new director has joined the board, potentially bringing fresh strategic insight and governance strength.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls see the buyback and acquisition as evidence that McBride is undervalued and ready for a rebound.  They argue the company’s stable contracts and expansion plans will lift margins and price.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the buyback is a sign of limited growth opportunities and that the Eurotab deal could dilute earnings if integration costs overrun expectations.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Community chatter has remained quiet but has gradually tilted slightly bullish as the buyback program progresses and the acquisition announcement surfaces.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat and shows no recent spikes, giving the stock a high surprise potential if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the second tranche of the buyback programme, which could lift the share price if the market interprets it as a sign of confidence.
Final closing of the Eurotab acquisition and the first earnings release after integration, which will reveal whether the deal adds value.
A positive earnings beat or a new large retail contract announcement that improves the outlook for private‑label sales.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume spike accompanied by a breakout above the 52‑week low, a clear earnings beat, or the announcement that the Eurotab integration is on track and cost‑effective.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early to mid‑stage of a crash‑recovery cycle.  The low trading range and the buyback suggest capitulation may already be largely complete, setting the stage for mean reversion if the catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: McBride’s share price is trading near the bottom of its recent range and the company has just added a buyback and a new acquisition that broaden its product line.  If the stock recovers to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the upside is roughly 50% from the current level, while the downside to a 20% drop is far smaller.  The asymmetric payoff is attractive for a contrarian who believes the market has over‑reacted to short‑term volatility.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the acquisition does not integrate smoothly or if cash burn remains high, the buyback may simply be a way to prop up the price without adding real value.  A continued earnings miss or a failure to secure new contracts could push the share further below its 52‑week low, exposing the trader to a 20% loss.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of about 50% versus a downside of roughly 20% – a 2.5:1 reward‑to‑risk ratio if the mean reversion plays out.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 52‑week low on volume at least 1.5× the average daily volume, and place a stop 10% below the entry point.  Position size should be limited to 5% of the portfolio to keep risk in check.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a clear earnings beat or a confirmed acquisition closing.  Watch for a 3‑day moving‑average cross or a 50‑day breakout as a confirmation signal.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy.  The only terminal risk would be a catastrophic earnings miss that forces a capital‑structure crisis.

ai_insights.bottom_line: McBride is a quietly oversold cleaning‑product maker that has just returned cash and expanded its product line, offering a potential mean‑reversion play with a 2.5:1 reward‑to‑risk profile if the stock climbs to the middle of its recent range.
